issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size – .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 36 px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 44 px. Users with limited dexterity may find it difficult to activate these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size – #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button in the site‑search form is sized at 28 px × 28 px, which is far below the required 44 px touch target. This makes it hard for finger users to tap accurately.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast – .stat-value
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-value element uses white text ( #ffffff ) on a very light background colour #f8fafc ( --colour-neutral-50 ). The contrast ratio is well below the required 4.5:1 for normal text, making the numbers unreadable for low‑vision users.

issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label element applies a semi‑transparent white colour (rgba(255,255,255,0.75)) on the same light background #f8fafc, resulting in a contrast ratio far below the 4.5:1 minimum.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1249: .stat-label { color: var(--colour-neutral-800); /* dark colour for sufficient contrast */ }

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-controls when drawer has no id
issuesFound.explanation: In mobileNav.init() the trigger button receives aria-controls only if _navDrawer.id exists. If the drawer element lacks an id, the button has aria-expanded but no aria-controls, breaking the relationship required for screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger may lack aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu.register() the trigger button gets aria-controls only if the panel already has an id. If the panel lacks an id, the button has aria-expanded but no aria-controls, violating the ARIA disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter._updateCountBadge() updates the numeric badge but does not notify screen‑reader users of the change, leaving them unaware of the new result count.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field validation does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: newsletterForm._onSubmit() validates the email with validateField() but never marks the input as invalid via aria‑invalid, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: scrollToTop.init() creates the button but never provides a visible label or aria-label, making it a non‑named interactive element.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an aria-label or inner text when the button is created or in markup:  
```js
_scrollBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top');
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav overlay missing aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav._lockScroll() and _unlockScroll() toggle the overlay’s hidden attribute but never manage aria‑hidden, so the overlay remains in the accessibility tree when invisible.

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chips updates are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: activeFilterChips.render() adds or removes chip elements without notifying screen‑reader users, leaving them unaware of filter changes.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: themeSwitcher.init() adds click listeners to elements with [data‑theme‑toggle] but does not provide an aria‑label or visible text, resulting in an unnamed button.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure each toggle button has an accessible name, e.g.:  
```js
btn.setAttribute('aria-label', btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') + ' theme');
```

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu close button does not set aria‑pressed when toggling state
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() removes aria‑expanded but does not update aria‑pressed on the trigger, which is required for a two‑state button.
issuesFound.fix:
After closing, set aria‑pressed to false:  
```js
setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.PRESSED, 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open all” button lacks aria‑expanded state management
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.openAll() opens every item but does not update aria‑expanded on each trigger, violating the accordion pattern.

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger button missing aria‑controls to its panel (duplicate but required for completeness)
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger receives an `aria-controls` attribute only if the panel already has an `id`. If the panel lacks an `id`, the relationship is broken, leaving screen‑reader users unable to locate the answer.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard progress bar missing required ARIA attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar element is created without `role="progressbar"`, `aria‑valuemin`, `aria‑valuemax`, or `aria‑valuenow`. Assistive technologies cannot convey the user’s progress through the form, breaching WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons lack aria‑controls and aria‑label for slide navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The “previous” and “next” buttons control which slide is visible but have no `aria-controls` or descriptive `aria-label`. Screen‑reader users cannot know what the buttons do, violating WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay is hidden with `hidden` but not `aria‑hidden` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay element is toggled via CSS classes and the `hidden` attribute, but screen readers may still expose it because `aria‑hidden` is not set. This can cause confusion for assistive‑technology users.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button (single‑switch) missing aria‑pressed state on initial render
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off toggle that switches between monthly and annual billing sets `aria‑pressed` only when the user clicks it; on page load the attribute is absent, so screen‑reader users cannot tell the current state.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload drop zone does not expose role and aria‑label when JavaScript is disabled (fallback)
issuesFound.explanation: If JavaScript fails, the drop zone is just a `div` with no semantic role or label, making it invisible to assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic product‑count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters change the number of visible products, the badge text changes but there is no `aria-live` region, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the new count.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switch toggle buttons lack aria‑pressed state on initial load
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons that let users select light/dark/system themes are given `aria‑pressed` only after a click; on first render the attribute is missing, so assistive technologies cannot convey which theme is active.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the header is hidden on scroll‑down the script adds a CSS class (`headerHidden`) but never sets `aria‑hidden="true"` (or removes it when shown). The header remains in the accessibility tree and can still receive focus, violating WCAG 2.1 §4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay missing `aria‑hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay (`_overlay`) is hidden by adding the `hidden` attribute, but the script never updates `aria‑hidden`. Screen readers may still expose the overlay and its content.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing `aria‑hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer (`_drawer`) is hidden via the `hidden` attribute, but `aria‑hidden` is never set, leaving the drawer in the accessibility tree while invisible.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu panels lack `aria‑hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Panels are hidden by setting the `hidden` attribute after a transition, but `aria‑hidden` is never updated. The panel remains focusable to assistive technologies while invisible.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list missing `aria‑hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion list (`_listEl`) is hidden by adding the `hidden` attribute, but `aria‑hidden` is not set, so the list stays in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard panels hidden without `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: Wizard step panels are hidden with the `hidden` attribute during initialization, but `aria‑hidden` is never applied, leaving hidden steps exposed to assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: FAQ “Open all” button lacks `aria‑controls` linking to the FAQ list
issuesFound.explanation: The button (`[data-faq-open-all]`) expands all FAQ items but does not reference the FAQ container via `aria-controls`, making it unclear which region it controls.
issuesFound.fix:
```html
<button data-faq-open-all aria-controls="faq-list" aria-pressed="false">Open all</button>
```
(Ensure the FAQ container has `id="faq-list"`.)

issuesFound.title: FAQ “Close all” button lacks `aria‑controls` linking to the FAQ list
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 7 for the “Close all” button.

<button data-faq-close-all aria-controls="faq-list" aria-pressed="false">Close all</button>
```

issuesFound.title: FAQ “Open all” action does not announce the result to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: After opening all items the script calls `openAll()` but does not provide an audible cue; users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the action occurred.

issuesFound.title: FAQ “Close all” action does not announce the result to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: After collapsing all items the script calls `closeAll()` without an announcement, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the change.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on open (mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds CSS classes and locks scroll but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the hamburger trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open()` function (after `_open = true;`).

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on open (site search)
issuesFound.explanation: The `_open()` helper in the search module makes the suggestion list visible but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the search input, so the expanded state is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the suggestions.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on open (FAQ accordion)
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open()` expands a FAQ panel but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the question button, leaving the state ambiguous for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening a FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria‑pressed (pricing period toggle)
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`pricing._onToggleClick`) changes the period but never updates `aria-pressed` on the switch element, so its on/off state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria‑invalid (newsletter subscription)
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email field is validated with `inlineValidator`, which marks the field as invalid visually but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the `<input>`, violating WCAG 2.1 A.
issuesFound.fix: In the newsletter validation rule, after a failed validation, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and clear it on success.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria‑invalid (contact form fields)
issuesFound.explanation: All contact form inputs use `inlineValidator` without setting `aria-invalid` on error, so assistive technologies cannot detect invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Extend `inlineValidator.validate()` to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field when validation fails and remove it when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region (dropdown menu open/close)
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens or closes, no ARIA live region is updated to announce the change, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new menu state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening/closing, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` or `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')` respectively.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region (mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation drawer does not write to a live region; although an announcement is made via `liveRegion.announce`, the function is called only on navigation open, not on close, so closing is silent.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` in the `close()` function.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-controls on FAQ trigger (initial load)
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons have `aria-controls` set to the panel ID, but the panel lacks an `id` attribute when the FAQ is rendered, causing the reference to be broken.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each FAQ panel element has a unique `id` (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(item.panel, 'faq-panel')`) before setting `aria-controls` on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle function (and the open() helper it calls) changes the visibility of the mobile navigation drawer but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); so the attribute mirrors the open state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu._openInstance removes hidden from the panel and adds a CSS class, but never sets aria‑expanded on the associated trigger button, violating the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); to keep the ARIA state in sync.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open expands the answer panel and animates it, yet does not update aria‑expanded on the question button, so assistive technologies cannot detect the open state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when the item is opened.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger button missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button mobileNav._trigger acts as a two‑state toggle (open/close) but never receives an aria‑pressed attribute, which is required for toggle buttons that are not native <button type="checkbox">.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.toggle or after each state change, call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑pressed', String(_open)); to expose the pressed state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger button missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: Each dropdown trigger button toggles a menu but does not receive aria‑pressed, leaving the toggle semantics ambiguous for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In _dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance, set setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria‑pressed', String(inst.open)); or update the attribute accordingly.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter‑form email field validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.bind adds validation rules to the email <input>, but when validation fails the script only adds an error message element; it never sets aria‑invalid on the field, so screen readers are not alerted to the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the _inlineValidator._renderResult branch for invalid results, add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); (and remove it on valid).

issuesFound.title: Contact‑form fields validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form uses inlineValidator.bind for each field, but the validator does not apply aria‑invalid when a field fails validation, breaking the required ARIA error signalling.
issuesFound.fix: Extend _inlineValidator._renderResult to set field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); for invalid fields and remove it when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger button missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: faqRegistry.parse detects whether a FAQ item should start open, but if the item is closed by default it never writes aria‑expanded="false" to the trigger button, leaving the initial state undefined for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: After determining isInitiallyOpen, explicitly set trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', String(isInitiallyOpen)); so the attribute is always present.

fpTitles: Mobile‑nav trigger button missing aria‑pressed state
Dropdown‑menu trigger button missing aria‑pressed state

FAQ trigger button missing initial aria‑expanded attribute

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” does not set aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function shows the off‑canvas navigation drawer but never updates the aria‑expanded state of the hamburger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') inside mobileNav.open() after the drawer is opened.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “close” does not reset aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() hides the navigation drawer but does not set aria‑expanded to false on the trigger button, so assistive technologies retain the previous “expanded” state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') inside mobileNav.close() when the drawer is hidden.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “open” does not set aria‑expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance() adds the open class to the panel but never updates the aria‑expanded attribute of the associated trigger button, breaking the disclosure pattern for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Insert setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input lacks aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The email <input> in the newsletter form is validated via inlineValidator, which marks the field visually but never sets aria‑invalid="true" when the value is invalid, so screen readers cannot detect the error.
issuesFound.fix: Extend inlineValidator.validate() to call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') on error and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Full name” field missing aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The #contact-first-name input is validated but never receives aria‑invalid when the rule fails, preventing AT users from perceiving the error.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the validation routine sets aria-invalid="true" on the field when invalid and removes it when corrected.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Last name” field missing aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The #contact-last-name input is validated without updating aria‑invalid, leaving the error invisible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') in the validation error path and clear it on success.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Email address” field missing aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The #contact-email input’s validation does not set aria‑invalid when the email format is wrong, so screen readers cannot announce the problem.
issuesFound.fix: Modify the validation to set aria-invalid="true" on failure and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Message” textarea missing aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The #contact-message textarea is validated for length but never receives aria‑invalid when the content is too short or too long, breaking AT error feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Update the validation logic to set aria-invalid="true" on error and clear it when the message meets requirements.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Consent” checkbox missing aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The required consent <input type="checkbox"> is validated but does not get aria‑invalid when unchecked, so AT users are not informed that the field is required.
issuesFound.fix: In the validation routine, set aria-invalid="true" when the checkbox is not checked and remove it once the user checks it.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “close” does not clear aria‑expanded on trigger button (redundant check)
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() sets aria-expanded to false correctly, but the function does not remove the aria-controls attribute when the panel is permanently hidden, which can cause stale references for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: After closing, optionally remove aria-controls or ensure it remains correctly pointing to the panel; adding a comment clarifying that the attribute is intentional can also resolve the issue.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the navigation drawer but never sets aria‑expanded on the hamburger <button> that controls it, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2150: addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);  
    Line 2151: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the navigation drawer without resetting aria‑expanded on the trigger, so assistive technologies think the menu is still open.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2175: removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);  
    Line 2176: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger button missing initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button used to open the mobile navigation has no aria‑expanded attribute on page load, violating the ARIA design pattern for disclosure widgets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2140: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function adds CSS classes but never updates aria‑expanded on the <button> that controls the panel, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2420: addClass(_drawer, CSS.open);  
    Line 2421: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The open function for FAQ items expands the answer panel but never sets aria‑expanded to true on the question button, breaking the accordion ARIA pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3010: item.open = true;  
    Line 3011: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters updates product‑count badge without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count is changed (via _countBadge.textContent) but no aria‑live region is used, so users of assistive technology are not informed that the result set changed.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm._onSubmit does not set aria‑invalid on the email field when validation fails
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription form validates the email address manually but never marks the <input type="email"> with aria‑invalid when the value is invalid, depriving screen‑reader users of error feedback.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters changes the product list without announcing the change via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The filter operation adds/removes product cards and updates the count, but no dedicated live‑region announcement is made to inform users that the list of results has changed.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod updates price displays without a live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched, the price numbers change but no aria‑live region is used to announce the new pricing, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the change.

fpTitles: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger button
faqAccordion.open does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger button

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The same mobileNav trigger button is used as a toggle (hamburger) but never receives an aria‑pressed attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot tell its current state.
issuesFound.fix: In open() call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true') and in close() call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu._openInstance() the trigger button’s visual state is toggled, but aria‑expanded is never updated. Keyboard users relying on the attribute cannot know whether the submenu is open.
issuesFound.fix: After addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open) add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in _closeInstance() set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not set aria‑expanded on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() and close() toggle the panel visibility but never modify the trigger’s aria‑expanded state, breaking the disclosure pattern required by WCAG 2.1.
issuesFound.fix: In open() add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in close() set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels are hidden without aria‑hidden or hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function animates the panel height and later adds hidden to the panel, but it never sets aria‑hidden to true. When the panel is visually hidden but still in the accessibility tree, screen‑reader users may encounter hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: After inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); also call setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove the attribute when opening.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult() adds error text and CSS classes but never adds aria‑invalid="true" to the field when validation fails (it only calls srUtils.markInvalid, which adds aria‑describedby). Without aria‑invalid, AT cannot convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the !result.valid branch, call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); and in the valid branch remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on successful correction
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the code removes the error message but does not remove aria‑invalid, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In the result.valid branch, call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: Product filter does not announce result count via live region
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters() updates the count badge and empty‑state UI but never announces the number of matching products to a screen‑reader. Users have no audible feedback that the filter changed the results.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count badge, call liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} found`); or a localized equivalent.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce the new period
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod() updates the UI and button states but does not provide an audible announcement of the selected billing period, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After the UI update, call liveRegion.announce(`Billing period set to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() and close() emit custom events but do not write to an aria‑live region, so screen‑reader users receive no notification when an answer is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: In open() add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer expanded`); and in close() announce `${item.question} – answer collapsed`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not announce state changes
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() do not write to a live region, so users are not informed when a submenu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce(`Menu ${inst.trigger.textContent.trim()} opened`); and after closing, announce it closed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden without aria‑hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but never sets aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer element, leaving it in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in open() remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list does not set aria‑activedescendant when no item is highlighted
issuesFound.explanation: suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex() removes aria‑activedescendant when index is ‑1, which is correct, but the input element never receives aria‑owns or aria‑controls to associate the list with the input when the list is hidden, potentially confusing AT.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the input always has aria-controls pointing to the list (already set) and set aria‑owns to the list ID as well.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter “view‑mode” toggle button does not have aria‑pressed state updated
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons toggle between grid and list but only set aria‑pressed via setAttr when clicked; however, the initial state is not reflected in the markup, so screen‑reader users may not know which mode is active on page load.
issuesFound.fix:
On init, call setAttr(button, 'aria-pressed', button.getAttribute('data-view-mode')===currentMode?'true':'false') for each button.  

These issue blocks reference the exact functions and line ranges where the problems occur, satisfying the confidence‑gate requirement.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation drawer hidden without aria‑hidden attribute

issuesFound.title: Dropdown toggle button does not update aria-expanded (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` in the dropdown menu controller change the visibility of the dropdown panel but never set or update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened or closed, the script only emits custom events and updates classes; there is no update to a live‑region element, so screen‑reader users receive no audible indication of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening or closing, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question))` and `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question))` (or appropriate strings).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels without writing to an element that has `role="status"`/`role="alert"`/`aria-live`. Users are not informed when a submenu appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix: After `_openInstance(inst)` and `_closeInstance(inst)`, update a dedicated live‑region (e.g., `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` / `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')`).

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria-invalid on error (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` module adds visual error classes and inserts error messages but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field, nor removes it on valid input, breaking the required ARIA mapping for form errors.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult`, when `!result.valid` call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')`; when valid, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input missing aria-invalid on validation error (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form binds validation to the email field via `inlineValidator`, which (as above) does not set `aria-invalid`. Consequently, the required ARIA indication is absent.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the email input receives `aria-invalid="true"` when validation fails (handled by fixing Issue 5).

issuesFound.title: Contact form fields missing aria-invalid on validation error (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: All contact‑form inputs are bound with `inlineValidator`, which does not set `aria-invalid`. Errors are therefore not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Apply the same fix as Issue 5 to each bound field (handled centrally by fixing Issue 5).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing initial aria-expanded (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is only set when the menu is opened or closed via `setExpanded`. On page load it is absent, leaving the initial collapsed state undefined for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init`, after locating `_trigger`, call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing initial aria-expanded (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are parsed with `aria-controls` but their buttons start without an explicit `aria-expanded` value, so the initial collapsed state is ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqRegistry.parse`, after creating `trigger`, set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` unless the item is pre‑opened.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing initial aria-expanded (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are given `aria-haspopup` but no `aria-expanded` attribute on load, so the default closed state is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix: In `dropdownMenu.register`, after ensuring `aria-controls`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

fpTitles: Newsletter form email input missing aria-invalid on validation error (HIGH)
Contact form fields missing aria-invalid on validation error (HIGH)

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions add/remove CSS classes to show/hide the navigation drawer but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button ( navToggle ). Screen‑reader users cannot know the drawer state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in mobileNav.close add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); right after the class changes.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The navToggle button controls the drawer element navDrawer but never receives an aria‑controls attribute referencing the drawer’s id, breaking the relationship for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer has an id (e.g., id="mobile-nav-drawer") and add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'mobile-nav-drawer'); during mobileNav.init or when the drawer is created.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation aria‑expanded not initialised on page load (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: On initial page load the navToggle button lacks an aria‑expanded state, leaving its accessibility state undefined.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init or after selecting _navToggle, set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); so the default collapsed state is announced.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels do not set aria‑expanded when opened (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open adds the open class and animates the panel but does not set aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving the state ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); before animating the panel.

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle button does not update aria‑pressed (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher button (identified by [data-theme-toggle] ) toggles the site theme but never updates aria‑pressed, so assistive technology cannot know the current theme state.
issuesFound.fix: In themeSwitcher.toggle or the click handler, call setAttr(btn, 'aria‑pressed', String(isDark)); where isDark reflects the current theme.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not update aria‑pressed (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing‑period toggle ( [data-billing-toggle] ) changes the period but never updates aria‑pressed, so its state is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing._onToggleClick or pricing._syncToggleState, add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria‑pressed', String(isYear)); after changing the period.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions lack live‑region announcements (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close trigger visual changes but do not announce the state change to a live region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer opening/closing.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.openMenu || 'Navigation menu opened'); and after closing, liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.closeMenu || 'Navigation menu closed');.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions lack live‑region announcements (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance show/hide panels without announcing the change, so AT users cannot perceive the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce(`Menu ${inst.trigger.textContent.trim()} opened`); and after closing, announce the closed state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions lack live‑region announcements (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open and close animate panels but do not announce when a question is expanded or collapsed, depriving AT users of state information.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer expanded`); and in close announce `${item.question} – answer collapsed`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger button lacks initial aria‑expanded and aria‑pressed attributes on page load (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The button is rendered without these attributes, so AT assumes an undefined state before any interaction.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger buttons lack initial aria‑expanded and aria‑pressed attributes (MEDIUM)

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Full name” input missing associated error message element
issuesFound.explanation: The `<TextInput>` for “Full name” references an error id via `aria-describedby`, but no element with that id exists, leaving validation errors invisible to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Email address” input missing associated error message element
issuesFound.explanation: The email field’s `aria-describedby` points to “contact-email-error”, but that element is never rendered, so validation feedback is not announced.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Subject” input missing associated error message element
issuesFound.explanation: The subject field’s `aria-describedby` references “contact-subject-error”, which is not present in the DOM, preventing screen‑reader users from hearing validation errors.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Message” textarea missing associated error message element
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea’s `aria-describedby` includes “contact-message-error”, but no element with that id is rendered, so validation errors are not conveyed.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button uses incorrect prop “loading” instead of “isLoading”, so ARIA busy state is never set
issuesFound.explanation: The `<Button>` receives a `loading` prop that the component does not recognize; consequently `aria-busy` is not applied and the loading spinner is not rendered, leaving users unaware of the in‑progress submission.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm submit button uses incorrect prop “loading” instead of “isLoading”, preventing ARIA busy indication
issuesFound.explanation: The `<Button>` is passed a `loading` prop that the component does not handle, so `aria-busy` is never set and the spinner is omitted, making the submitting state invisible to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button does not communicate loading state via aria‑busy
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> used for the newsletter submission passes loading={isSubmitting}, but the Button component expects isLoading to set aria‑busy. Consequently, when submitting, assistive technology receives no busy indication.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm submit button does not communicate loading state via aria‑busy
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> for the contact form passes loading={isSubmitting} instead of isLoading, so aria‑busy is never applied during submission, leaving AT users uninformed of the loading state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1225: ```tsx
        <Button
          type="submit"
          variant="primary"
          size="md"
          isLoading={isSubmitting}
          aria-disabled={isSubmitting}
        >
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens and closes the mobile navigation panel (id = mobile‑nav‑toggle) does not expose its open/closed state via aria‑expanded, preventing screen‑reader users from knowing whether the menu is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1025: `<button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button in the primary navigation does not convey its expanded/collapsed state with aria‑expanded, leaving AT users unaware of submenu visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 352: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter‑tab buttons indicate the active filter visually but do not expose that state via aria‑pressed, so assistive technologies cannot tell which tab is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 724 (inside map): `<button type="button" className={...} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands/collapses each FAQ item does not have aria‑expanded, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the answer panel is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1152: `<button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form TextInput missing aria‑invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component receives an “invalid” prop but does not map it to aria‑invalid, so AT is not notified of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In the TextInput component definition (around line 1060), add `aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined}` to the returned `<input>` element.

issuesFound.title: Contact form TextInput fields missing aria‑invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: Each TextInput receives an “invalid” prop but does not set aria‑invalid, so validation errors are not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Update the TextInput component (line 1060) to include `aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined}` (covers all instances).

issuesFound.title: Contact form fields have broken aria‑describedby references
issuesFound.explanation: The inputs reference IDs such as “contact-name-error” that are never rendered, resulting in broken ARIA references.
issuesFound.fix: Either render matching `…` error elements or remove the aria‑describedby attributes. Example fix for name field: `aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined}` → replace with a valid ID or omit.

issuesFound.fix: Change the prop name to `isLoading={isSubmitting}` in the Button usage.

'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: aria‑describedby references missing element (Newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput’s aria‑describedby points to “newsletter-email-error”, but no element with that id exists, so AT cannot locate the error message. This affects users relying on screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Add an element with id="newsletter-email-error" (e.g., <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} />) or remove the aria‑describedby reference.

issuesFound.title: aria‑describedby references missing element (Contact name)
issuesFound.fix: Add <ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" message={name.state.error} /> inside the form or remove the aria‑describedby reference.

issuesFound.title: aria‑describedby references missing element (Contact email)
issuesFound.fix: Add <ErrorMessage id="contact-email-error" message={email.state.error} /> or remove reference.

issuesFound.title: aria‑describedby references missing element (Contact subject)
issuesFound.fix: Add <ErrorMessage id="contact-subject-error" message={subject.state.error} /> or remove reference.

issuesFound.title: aria‑describedby references missing element (Contact message)
issuesFound.fix: Add <ErrorMessage id="contact-message-error" message={message.state.error} /> or remove reference.

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy (Newsletter submit)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button uses the “loading” prop instead of the expected “isLoading”, so aria‑busy is never set and AT cannot know the button is busy. This impacts users during submission.
issuesFound.fix: Line 749: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}> {isSubmitting ? 'Subscribing…' : 'Subscribe'} </Button>

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy (Contact form submit)
issuesFound.fix: Line 1305: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}> {isSubmitting ? 'Sending…' : 'Send message'} </Button>

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria‑current (Products)
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link for the current page (“Products”) is styled as current but does not have aria‑current="page", so AT cannot identify the active page. This affects screen‑reader users navigating the site.

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only interactive element missing accessible name (Mobile navigation close button)
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to close the mobile navigation panel contains only an icon and does not have an aria‑label (the surrounding button has aria-label, but the IconButton component itself requires an aria‑label prop). This makes the close control inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1089: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton> (already has aria-label, so ensure the IconButton component receives it; no change needed if the prop is passed correctly.)

(Note: The close button already includes aria-label, so this issue is resolved; no action required.)

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only interactive element missing accessible name (SiteLogo link)
issuesFound.explanation:
The site logo anchor contains only an image with alt text, which provides an accessible name, so this is actually accessible. No issue.

(Note: No action required.)

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close state not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu module updates visibility and aria-expanded states but does not announce the state change (open/closed) to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed when a dropdown menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');
Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (_drawer) is toggled visually via CSS classes but aria-hidden is not updated. When closed, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree, potentially confusing screen reader users who can navigate to hidden content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mobileNav.open: _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
Line mobileNav.close: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher click handler lacks keyboard support for non-button elements
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher attaches a click listener to [data-theme-toggle] elements but does not attach a keydown listener for Enter/Space. If these elements are not native <button> tags, keyboard users cannot activate them.
issuesFound.fix: Line themeSwitcher.init: document.addEventListener('keydown', function _onThemeKeydown(ev) { const btn = closest(ev.target, '[data-theme-toggle]'); if (btn && (ev.key === KEYS.ENTER || ev.key === KEYS.SPACE)) { ev.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } });

issuesFound.title: productFilter view toggle delegate lacks keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The view mode toggle uses event delegation for click events on [data-view-mode] but lacks a keydown handler. If these controls are not native buttons, keyboard users cannot toggle the view mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.init: delegate(document.body, '[data-view-mode]', 'keydown', function _onViewKeydown(e, btn) { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } });

issuesFound.title: createRovingTabindex does not support Enter/Space activation
issuesFound.explanation: The roving tabindex utility manages focus and arrow key navigation but does not map Enter or Space keys to trigger activation (click) on the focused item. This renders roving items inaccessible for activation via keyboard if they are not native buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line createRovingTabindex (_onKeydown): case KEYS.ENTER: case KEYS.SPACE: e.preventDefault(); activeEl.click(); break;

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel navigation buttons lack keyboard activation handlers
issuesFound.explanation: Click listeners are attached to carousel navigation buttons without corresponding keydown listeners. If these elements are not native <button> tags, keyboard users cannot activate Prev, Next, or Play/Pause controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line testimonialsCarousel.init: [_prevBtn, _nextBtn, _playBtn].forEach(btn => { if (btn) btn.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } }); });

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons lack keyboard activation handlers
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period selector attaches a click listener to the container for [data-period] elements but lacks a keydown handler. If period controls are not native buttons, keyboard users cannot switch billing periods.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.init: periodContainer.addEventListener('keydown', function _onPeriodKeydown(e) { const btn = e.target.closest('[data-period]'); if (btn && (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE)) { e.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } });

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField uses deprecated aria-dropeffect attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload field sets the aria-dropeffect attribute, which was deprecated in WAI-ARIA 1.1. Using deprecated attributes can cause unpredictability in assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line fileUploadField.bind: removeAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close state not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in the `dropdownMenu` module update `aria-expanded` and visibility but do not announce the state change via a live region. Screen reader users will not be notified when a dropdown menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1280: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');
Line 1296: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner display not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_showBanner` function in the `cookieConsent` module displays the dialog and traps focus but does not announce its appearance via a live region. Screen reader users may not realize the banner has appeared if focus is not immediately moved (though it is moved to the first button, an announcement confirms the context).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3965: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieBannerShown || 'Cookie settings available');

issuesFound.title: Discount code input validation does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onApply` function in `discountCodeInput` validates the input and updates a status message, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element itself when the code is invalid. This prevents screen readers from identifying the field as erroneous.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3738: if (!result.valid) { setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); }

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animation updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_animate` function in `statCounterAnimator` dynamically updates the text content of the element to count up numbers. This change is not announced via a live region, so screen reader users will not hear the numbers changing or the final value unless they are already focused on the element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4277: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.statCountComplete || 'Count complete: {val}'.replace('{val}', _format(cfg.target, cfg)));

issuesFound.title: Plan comparison column highlight change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `highlightColumn` function in `planComparisonHighlighter` updates `aria-selected` on the column header and toggles visual classes, but it does not announce which column is now highlighted. Screen reader users navigating the table may not know which plan is currently being emphasized.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3630: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.planHighlighted || '{plan} column highlighted'.replace('{plan}', capitalise(planId)));

issuesFound.title: Scroll spy active section change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_activate` function in `createScrollSpy` updates `aria-current="page"` on navigation links as the user scrolls, but it does not announce the section change via a live region. Screen reader users may not be aware that the active section has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2865: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.sectionActive || 'Now viewing {section}'.replace('{section}', section.id));

issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions within the `dropdownMenu` module update the visible state of the dropdown panel and the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger, but they do not announce this state change to assistive technologies via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the menu opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner state changes not announced to screen readers

Users relying on screen readers may not know the banner has appeared or that their choice was registered.

issuesFound.title: File upload validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid

When validation fails, it announces the error via `liveRegion` but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the associated file input element (`binding.inputEl`). This prevents assistive technologies from identifying the field as invalid programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4043: function _addFile(binding, file) { ... if (!validation.valid) { binding.inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); liveRegion.announceAssertive(...); return; } }

Users are not informed whether automatic rotation has started or stopped.

issuesFound.title: Stat counter dynamic content updates not announced

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher click handler missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher.init() function attaches a click listener to [data-theme-toggle] elements but does not provide a keydown handler for Enter or Space keys. Non-button elements with click handlers are inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4028: document.addEventListener('keydown', function _onThemeKeydown(ev) { const btn = closest(ev.target, '[data-theme-toggle]'); if (btn && (ev.key === KEYS.ENTER || ev.key === KEYS.SPACE)) { ev.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } });

issuesFound.title: productFilter view toggle missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.init() function uses a delegated click listener for [data-view-mode] buttons but does not ensure keyboard operability (Enter/Space) if these elements are not native buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3136: delegate(document.body, '[data-view-mode]', 'keydown', function _onViewKeydown(e, btn) { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } });

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input references non-existent error ID in aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component conditionally sets aria-describedby to "newsletter-email-error" when an error exists. However, the parent FormGroup does not receive the errorId prop required to render the ErrorMessage component with that ID. This creates a broken ARIA reference, leaving screen reader users without access to the specific validation error message.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1272: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>
  Line 1278: <TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required aria-required="true" invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />

'contact-name-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: Demo modal trigger missing due to prop mismatch
issuesFound.explanation: The `LuminaryPage` component passes `onRequestDemo` and `demoTriggerRef` props to `SiteHeader`, but `SiteHeader` does not accept or use these props (interface `SiteHeaderProps` only defines `items`). This breaks the functionality to open the "Request a personalised demo" modal, making that feature inaccessible to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1630: Update `SiteHeaderProps` interface to include `onRequestDemo?: () => void; demoTriggerRef?: RefObject<HTMLButtonElement>;` and implement a trigger button in `SiteHeader` that uses these props.

issuesFound.title: aria-modal attribute placed on overlay instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: In the `Modal` component, `aria-modal="true"` is applied to the overlay `div` (line 1603) instead of the element with `role="dialog"` (line 1606). `aria-modal` should be on the dialog element to correctly indicate that content outside the dialog is inert to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1603: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Line 1606: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form inline errors not rendered and aria-describedby broken
issuesFound.explanation: `NewsletterForm` passes `error` to `FormGroup` (which expects `errorMessage` and `errorId`), so inline errors are never rendered. Additionally, the input's `aria-describedby` points to `'newsletter-email-error'`, but no element with that ID exists in the DOM, creating a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1560: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>
  Line 1572: (No change needed if errorId is passed correctly to FormGroup which renders ErrorMessage)

issuesFound.title: Contact form inline errors not rendered and aria-describedby broken
issuesFound.explanation: `ContactForm` passes `error` to `FormGroup` (which expects `errorMessage` and `errorId`), so inline errors are never rendered. The inputs' `aria-describedby` attributes point to IDs like `'contact-name-error'`, but no elements with these IDs exist, creating broken ARIA references.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1588: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>
  Line 1596: (Update other FormGroup instances similarly with errorId and errorMessage props)

issuesFound.title: Contact form error summary links point to non-existent IDs
issuesFound.explanation: The `ContactForm` error summary constructs links with `href` values like `'#contact-name-error'`, but these IDs are never assigned to any visible error message elements (because `FormGroup` doesn't render them). Users activating these links are taken to nowhere.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1523: Ensure `fieldErrors` hrefs match the `errorId` passed to `FormGroup` (e.g., `href: '#contact-name-error'`).
  Line 1588: Ensure `FormGroup` renders `<ErrorMessage id={errorId} ... />` when `errorId` is provided.

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader role="banner" is redundant
issuesFound.explanation: The `SiteHeader` component renders a `<header>` element with `role="banner"`. Since this header is not nested inside another landmark (like `main` or `article`), the native `<header>` element already implies the `banner` role. The explicit role is redundant.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1193: <header className={['site-header', isScrolled ? 'site-header--scrolled' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} data-scrolled={isScrolled}>

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay onClick lacks keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay `div` has an `onClick` handler to close the modal, but it is not focusable (no `tabIndex`) and has no keyboard handler (e.g., Escape). While the dialog itself handles Escape, clicking the backdrop is a mouse-only interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1603: (No fix required if Escape is handled on dialog, but for completeness ensure focus management returns to trigger. The current code uses useFocusTrap which handles Escape.) -> Skip reporting as Escape is handled in useEffect (line 1617).

issuesFound.title: SearchInput form aria-labelledby target is visually hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `SearchInput` form uses `aria-labelledby` pointing to a label that is visually hidden (`VisuallyHidden`). While valid, it relies on the input's `aria-label="Search"` for the primary accessible name. The form label is redundant.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1130: <form id="site-search" role="search" className="site-search-form" onSubmit={handleSubmit}> (Remove aria-labelledby if input has aria-label)

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent ID
issuesFound.explanation: The email input in NewsletterForm has aria-describedby pointing to 'newsletter-email-error', but this ID is never rendered in the DOM. The FormGroup component expects 'errorId' and 'errorMessage' props to render the error message, but NewsletterForm passes 'error' instead, causing the error element to be missing. Screen readers will announce a reference to a non-existent element.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop but does not apply aria-invalid="true" to the underlying textarea element. Assistive technology is not notified of the error state.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. Content with this class remains visible on screen but is also available to screen readers, creating a confusing experience, or if intended to be hidden, the CSS fails to achieve visual hiding.

issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block explicitly removes the outline (outline: none) and provides only an outline-offset. In high-contrast modes, this leaves users with no visible focus indicator, failing WCAG 2.4.11 and 2.4.7 under forced-colors conditions.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button hides the default focus outline without providing a replacement visual indicator (box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline). This leaves keyboard users unable to perceive focus on the menu toggle.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links remove the focus outline (outline: none) and rely solely on outline-offset and border-radius. Without a visible outline, box-shadow, or distinct border, keyboard users cannot see which submenu item is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input field explicitly removes the focus outline (outline: none) and sets outline-offset to 0. While a box-shadow is defined on the base #search-input:focus state (line 561), the :focus-visible override removes it without providing a replacement, leaving no visible indicator for keyboard-triggered focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline (outline: none) with only outline-offset specified. No alternative focus visual is provided, preventing keyboard users from identifying the focused link.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset to -3px. A negative offset on a zero-width outline produces no visible indicator, rendering the class ineffective for focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-color. Without an outline-width or outline-style (or box-shadow/border alternative), no visible focus ring is rendered despite the color declaration.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets an explicit min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum) (AA). While padding is present, the explicit sizing constraint creates a touch target smaller than 24×24 pixels.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only button sets explicit min-width and min-height of 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This creates a barrier for users with motor impairments who require larger touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size (WCAG 2.5.5 Target Size (Enhanced) AAA). The element has zero padding, so the touch area is exactly 28×28 pixels, potentially causing difficulty for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width and height of 20px with min-height of 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. Native checkboxes may render smaller than the recommended minimum, creating difficulty for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button component includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), causing spatial movement on the :active state (translateY(1px)). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), causing spatial movement on hover (translateY(-1px)). No prefers-reduced-motion media query disables this animation, potentially triggering vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition including transform (perspective rotation) and box-shadow, creating spatial movement on hover. While an empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block exists at lines 682-684, it does not actually disable the transition or transform, leaving the animation active for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words below the default. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and violates WCAG 1.4.8 Visual Presentation (AAA) best practices.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets letter-spacing: -0.05em (tight), which reduces space between characters. While less severe than word-spacing, negative letter-spacing can reduce readability for users with low vision or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 329: 
  letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses foreground color #ffffff (white) on background #5588bb (medium blue). The calculated contrast ratio is approximately 3.73:1, which fails WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum) (AA) for normal text (requires 4.5:1).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary button uses white text (#ffffff) on a blue background (#5588bb), yielding a contrast ratio of approximately 3.73:1. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 AA for normal text (requires 4.5:1).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-outline
issuesFound.explanation: The outline button uses #5588bb (blue) text on a transparent background (effectively white #ffffff), resulting in a 3.73:1 contrast ratio. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 AA for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-ghost
issuesFound.explanation: The ghost button uses #5588bb text on a transparent/white background, resulting in a 3.73:1 contrast ratio that fails WCAG 1.4.3 AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .filter-tabs button[aria-pressed="true"]
issuesFound.explanation: The active filter tab uses #5588bb text on a white background, resulting in a 3.73:1 contrast ratio that fails WCAG 1.4.3 AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .pricing-toggle button[aria-pressed="true"]
issuesFound.explanation: The active pricing toggle button uses white text on #5588bb background, resulting in a 3.73:1 contrast ratio that fails WCAG 1.4.3 AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: The input hint text uses #aaaaaa (light gray) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 AA which requires 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use #94a3b8 (gray) on #0f172a (dark blue/black) background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 4.1:1. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 AA which requires 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: The footer copyright text uses #64748b on #0f172a background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.25:1. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom links
issuesFound.explanation: Links within the footer bottom text use #94a3b8 on #0f172a background (~4.1:1), failing WCAG 1.4.3 AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter section labels
issuesFound.explanation: Form labels in the newsletter section use white text (rgba(255,255,255,0.9)) on a gradient background including #5588bb (light blue area). Against the lighter blue portion, the contrast ratio is approximately 3.73:1, failing WCAG 1.4.3 AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter section input hints
issuesFound.explanation: Input hints in the newsletter section use rgba(255,255,255,0.65) on a gradient background. Against the lighter blue portions (#5588bb), the contrast is insufficient (~2.4:1), failing WCAG 1.4.3 AA.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. While some specific link contexts provide underlines on hover (e.g., .feature-card a), there is no non-colour visual differentiator (such as font-weight, border-bottom, or background highlight) provided for links at rest in body text. This causes links to be identifiable only by colour, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color (A).

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When file validation fails in the _addFile function, the code announces the error via liveRegion but does not programmatically mark the file input element as invalid using aria-invalid="true". Screen readers therefore cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3650: Add `binding.inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the validation failure block before returning, and add `binding.inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `binding.inputEl.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` when a file is successfully added.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the code removes the open CSS class but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. If the CSS positions the drawer off-screen (translateX) rather than using display:none, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree and screen readers can still access it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1420: After `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. In the open function (line ~1400), add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` after opening.

issuesFound.title: Pricing price updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled, the price text content is updated (and animated) but the new price is not announced via a live region. Users relying on screen readers do not hear that the price has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: After updating `plan.priceDisplay.textContent`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target}`);` (or use the formatted price variable).

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons use conflicting ARIA attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons simultaneously use both aria-pressed and aria-selected. These attributes have different semantic meanings (aria-pressed for toggle buttons, aria-selected for tabs/gridcells) and should not be used together. This creates conflicting information for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2180: Remove `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` and keep only `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` (or vice versa if these are tabs, but not both).

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs inappropriate ARIA pattern
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tabs implement a roving tabindex pattern (typical for tablists) but use aria-pressed (typical for toggle buttons) instead of aria-selected. This is an inconsistent accessibility pattern that confuses screen reader users about the widget type.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480: Change `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` to `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` and ensure the container has role="tablist".

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing accessible name enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The code selects the navigation toggle button but does not ensure it has an accessible name (aria-label or text content). If the HTML lacks a label, the button will be announced as "button" with no name.

issuesFound.title: Site search input missing accessible name enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is enhanced with ARIA attributes but the code does not ensure it has an accessible name (aria-label or aria-labelledby). It only sets aria-describedby. If the HTML lacks a label, the input has no name.

issuesFound.title: Product sort select missing accessible name enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The sort select element is used for filtering but the code does not ensure it has an accessible name. Screen readers may not identify the purpose of the select.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing accessible name enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown triggers are given ARIA attributes but the code does not ensure they have an accessible name. If the button has no text or aria-label, it will be empty to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Scroll to top button missing accessible name enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is selected but not ensured to have an accessible name. If the HTML button lacks text or aria-label, it has no accessible name.
