issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to display the mobile navigation drawer and emits an event, but it never sets the triggering button’s aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers will continue to report the menu as collapsed even though it is visually open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2156: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment inside the open function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds an open class to the panel, but it does not set the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute to "true". This leaves assistive technology unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the _openInstance function, immediately after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands a FAQ answer panel and updates visual state, but it never sets the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute to "true". Because the close() function does set it to "false", the attribute remains stale when an item is opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3750: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` inside the open function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field fails validation, markInvalid() adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1682: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the null checks and before the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav module does not activate focus trap when opening drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a _trap variable intended for focus trapping, but the open() function never initializes or activates the trap. Consequently, keyboard focus can leave the drawer and move to background content, and focus is not returned to the trigger button when the drawer closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2158 (inside open function): Insert `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` immediately after `_lockScroll();`.
  Line 2195 (inside close function): Ensure `_trap.deactivate();` is called (the check exists but the trap is never active).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens or closes, no announcement is made via the liveRegion. Users navigating with screen readers receive no feedback that the menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2287 (end of _openInstance): Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`.
  Line 2312 (end of _closeInstance): Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader.hide() fails to hide header from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The hide() function visually hides the sticky header via CSS classes (likely translating it off-screen), but it does not set aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users can still navigate to interactive elements inside the hidden header.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2484: Insert `_header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden);`.
  Line 2499 (inside show function): Insert `_header.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` immediately after `removeClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer lacks aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer acts as a modal dialog (obscures page content and traps focus), but the code does not set aria-modal="true" on the drawer element. Assistive technologies may not treat it as a modal surface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2160: Insert `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-modal', 'true');` inside the open function, immediately after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: characterCounter.attach() fails to link counter to input via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The function creates a live character-count element and inserts it into the DOM, but it never associates the counter with the input field using aria-describedby. Screen readers do not automatically read the character count when the field is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3428: After creating counterEl, insert `counterEl.id = srUtils.ensureId(field, 'counter') + '-count';` and then `field.setAttribute('aria-describedby', counterEl.id);` (ensuring any existing describedby values are preserved).

mobileNav drawer lacks aria-modal attribute

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function toggles CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers will continue to announce the menu as collapsed even when it is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2845: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;` inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function updates the panel visibility but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves assistive technologies unaware that the menu is expanded.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function adds an error description via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies cannot convey the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function removes the mobile navigation drawer but does not announce the state change to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology receive no confirmation that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2880: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately before the closing brace of the close() function (after the emit call).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu reveals new content but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users may be unaware that the menu has appeared.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement

issuesFound.fix: Line 3110: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` at the end of the _closeInstance() function, before the emit() call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not activated when opening
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a focus trap variable but never initializes or activates it when the menu opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background page, breaking the modal experience.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion panel close function hides the listbox but does not announce the closure to screen readers. Users may not realize the suggestions have disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3950: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.searchClosed || 'Search suggestions closed');` inside the _close() function, after setting `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher missing initial aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher sets aria-pressed when buttons are clicked, but does not initialize the attribute on page load. Screen readers cannot indicate which theme is currently active on initial render.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons lack aria-pressed state

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput._onApply() omits aria-invalid on validation failure

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField._addFile() omits aria-invalid on validation failure
issuesFound.explanation: When a file fails validation (size or type), the function announces the error via liveRegion.announceAssertive but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the file input element. Assistive technologies will not identify the input as having an invalid value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4785: Add `setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the `if (!validation.valid)` block before the return statement.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is not updated to "true". Screen readers cannot convey the expanded state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not set aria-expanded="false" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is not reset to "false". Screen readers will continue to announce the button as expanded, misleading users about the current state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not set aria-expanded="false" on trigger

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions lack live-region announcements

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers open and close panels but do not update the aria-expanded attribute to indicate the state change to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `inst.open = true;`
  Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When expanding an FAQ item, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true", though the close function correctly sets it to "false". This asymmetry prevents screen readers from detecting when an item is opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox trigger for the suggestion panel but does not update aria-expanded when the panel opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line siteSearch._open: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line siteSearch._close: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) indicate active state via CSS classes but do not expose this state to screen readers via aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing._syncToggleState: Insert `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The form validation utility marks fields as invalid by linking error messages with aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true", so screen readers cannot identify the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never created
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap, leaving keyboard users able to tab out of the open mobile menu into the background page.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init: Insert `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` immediately after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clear
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors, the utility removes aria-describedby references but does not remove or set to "false" the aria-invalid attribute, leaving stale invalid states on fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markValid: Insert `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` at the end of the function before the closing brace

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on page load, leaving screen readers unaware that the menu is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `_trigger.addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are initialized with aria-haspopup and aria-controls but not aria-expanded="false", failing to indicate the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu.register: Insert `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input initializes with aria-controls but not aria-expanded="false", failing to indicate the initial collapsed state of the suggestion panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line suggestionListRenderer.mount: Insert `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter result changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but no aria-live announcement informs screen reader users of the new result count or that filtering is complete.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` immediately after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates prices visually but does not announce the change to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);` immediately after `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the scroll-to-top button scrolls the page to the top but does not notify screen reader users that the action occurred.
issuesFound.fix: Line scrollToTop._onClick: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');` immediately after `window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' });`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer does not trigger a live region announcement to inform screen reader users of the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mobileNav.open: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` immediately after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`
  Line mobileNav.close: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus does not announce the state change to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` immediately after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`
  Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` immediately after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding and collapsing FAQ items does not announce the action or the item title to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line faqAccordion.open: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);` immediately after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });`
  Line faqAccordion.close: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);` immediately after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`

issuesFound.title: contactForm fields missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form name and email inputs lack autocomplete attributes, preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill and violating WCAG 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line contactForm.init: After `const contactFirstName = qs('[name="full_name"]', _formEl);` add `if (contactFirstName) contactFirstName.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`
  Line contactForm.init: After `const contactEmail = qs('[name="email"]', _formEl);` add `if (contactEmail) contactEmail.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input lacks autocomplete="email", preventing password managers and browsers from filling the field automatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line newsletterForm.init: After `_emailEl = qs('input[type="email"]', _formEl);` add `if (_emailEl) _emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard fields missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard form fields for name, email, and card name lack autocomplete attributes (name, email, cc-name), hindering autofill accessibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line multiStepWizard.init: After parsing steps, add `const wizardName = qs('[name="wizard_name"]', _form); if (wizardName) wizardName.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`
  Line multiStepWizard.init: Add `const wizardEmail = qs('[name="wizard_email"]', _form); if (wizardEmail) wizardEmail.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`
  Line multiStepWizard.init: Add `const wizardCardName = qs('[name="wizard_card_name"]', _form); if (wizardCardName) wizardCardName.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'cc-name');`

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard progress bar missing aria-valuemin/max
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar element has aria-valuenow but lacks aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax, which are required for screen readers to interpret the progress value correctly.
issuesFound.fix: Line multiStepWizard._updateProgress: Insert `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0');` and `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100');` before the line setting aria-valuenow.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close instance functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions show and hide dropdown panels but do not synchronise the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button, violating the disclosure widget pattern required by WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1312: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `_openInstance` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 1329: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside `_closeInstance` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the `open` function omits setting `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger (the `close` function correctly sets it to "false"). This leaves screen readers announcing the panel as collapsed when it is visually open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3857: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on validation failure
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function marks invalid fields visually and links error messages via `aria-describedby`, but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input. Screen readers rely on this attribute to programmatically convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4558: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the `else` block (invalid state), immediately after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on validation success
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, `markValid` removes the error description but leaves `aria-invalid="true"` set on the element, incorrectly signalling an error to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4548: Add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` inside the `if (result.valid)` block, immediately after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with `aria-expanded="false"` when the module initialises, leaving screen readers unaware that the drawer is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1278: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_initialised = true;` in the `init` function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are registered and given `aria-controls` and `aria-haspopup`, but lack the initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute required to indicate the panel is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1350: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside the `register` function, immediately after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox input missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox controlling a suggestion listbox, but the `mount` function in `suggestionListRenderer` omits setting the initial `aria-expanded="false"` required for combobox widgets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2262: Add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` in the `mount` function.

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle does not announce changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function dynamically updates price values and billing labels when users switch between monthly/annual plans, but no `aria-live` announcement notifies screen reader users of the change in billing period or price.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3072: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged.replace('{period}', BILLING_PERIODS[period].label));` (or equivalent string) after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce result changes
issuesFound.explanation: When filtering products, the `applyFilters` function visually shows/hides cards and updates the count badge, but fails to announce the result count or filter status via a live region, leaving screen reader users unaware the grid has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2758: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products match the selected filter`);` (using appropriate template string) inside the `filterAnimation.run` callback, immediately after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close does not announce state changes
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes visible content without announcing the action to screen readers, preventing users from knowing that the disclosure widget responded to their input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3868: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question));` at the end of the `open` function. Line 3892: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question));` at the end of the `close` function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close does not announce drawer state
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation drawer via `open` and `close` functions changes the visual UI but provides no `aria-live` announcement to confirm the menu is now open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1230: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen);` at the end of the `open` function. Line 1260: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed);` at the end of the `close` function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close does not announce menu state
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions reveal or hide dropdown menus without announcing the state change via a live region, leaving screen reader users uncertain whether the menu is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1318: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened');` at the end of `_openInstance`. Line 1338: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');` at the end of `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField does not set aria-invalid on validation failure
issuesFound.explanation: When `_addFile` rejects a file for size or type violations, it announces the error via a live region but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the file input element, failing to programmatically mark the field as erroneous.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6058: Add `setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the `if (!validation.valid)` block, immediately before the `return;` statement.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates

issuesFound.title: pricing switch missing aria-checked attribute

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() and markValid() helper functions (used by inlineValidator) manage error message associations via aria-describedby, but they fail to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields and aria-invalid="false" (or remove the attribute) on valid fields, preventing assistive technologies from identifying invalid inputs.

Line 1927: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside markInvalid() after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`
  Line 1943: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` inside markValid() after removing the describedby attribute

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function selects the trigger button and drawer elements but does not initialize the aria-expanded attribute to "false" on the trigger, even though the drawer is closed by default on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2462: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after the line `_initialised = true;` in the init() function

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets up aria-haspopup and aria-controls on trigger buttons, but it does not initialize aria-expanded to "false" for dropdowns that are closed by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2660: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after the line `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` in the register() function

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount() function configures the search input as a combobox with aria-controls and aria-haspopup, but it does not initialize aria-expanded to "false" for the closed suggestion list state.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not establish the programmatic relationship between the navigation toggle button and the drawer panel by setting the aria-controls attribute on the trigger to point to the drawer's ID.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2458: Add `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav');` to ensure the drawer has an ID, then add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);` after selecting the elements in init()

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing keyboard users to tab into background content. The code references a _trap variable but never initializes or activates a focus trap in the open() function.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2472: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` and `_trap.activate();` inside open() after `_lockScroll();`
  Line 2495: Ensure `_trap.deactivate();` is called in close() (currently conditional on `_trap.isActive()`, but _trap is never assigned)

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when validation fails, but the FormGroup component wrapping it does not receive an errorId prop. Consequently, no element with id "newsletter-email-error" exists in the DOM, breaking the association. Screen readers will not announce the error message to users.

issuesFound.title: Contact form name input references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The name input has aria-describedby="contact-name-error" when validation fails, but the FormGroup component wrapping it does not receive an errorId prop. No element with id "contact-name-error" exists in the DOM. Screen readers will not announce the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: Change `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>` to `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has aria-describedby="contact-email-error" when validation fails, but the FormGroup component wrapping it does not receive an errorId prop. No element with id "contact-email-error" exists in the DOM. Screen readers will not announce the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220: Change `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>` to `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form subject input references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input has aria-describedby="contact-subject-error" when validation fails, but the FormGroup component wrapping it does not receive an errorId prop. No element with id "contact-subject-error" exists in the DOM. Screen readers will not announce the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: Change `<FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required error={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>` to `<FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" error={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form message textarea references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea has aria-describedby including "contact-message-error" when validation fails, but the FormGroup component wrapping it does not receive an errorId prop. No element with id "contact-message-error" exists in the DOM. Screen readers will not announce the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1260: Change `<FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>` to `<FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} errorId="contact-message-error" error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog aria-modal attribute on incorrect element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div (the backdrop) instead of the element with role="dialog". According to ARIA specifications, aria-modal should be set on the dialog element itself to correctly indicate the modal boundary to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1400: Remove `aria-modal="true"` from `<div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose}>`. Line 1402: Add `aria-modal="true"` to `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference

This creates a broken ARIA reference that points to a non-existent element.
issuesFound.fix: Pass errorId="newsletter-email-error" and errorMessage={email.state.error} to the FormGroup component, or remove the aria-describedby attribute from the TextInput when no error element is present.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div onClick without keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div handles click events to close the modal but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers for Enter and Space. Keyboard-only users cannot activate this control.
issuesFound.fix: <div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" role="button" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div onClick without keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation backdrop div handles click events to close the menu but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard-only users cannot activate this control.
issuesFound.fix: <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" aria-hidden="true" role="button" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }}>
